verbatim_text: Okay, so we're going to train language models to explain their internal mechanisms in two parts. First, we're going to extract self descriptions from interrupt tools. And second, we're going to train language models on those descriptions using SFT. So starting from the first step, we extract answers to three types of questions about model internals. What do your internal activations represent? What internal components did you use to make a decision? And what input components did you use to make a decision? So we've already seen inter procedures for two of these three questions. So this first question can be answered through probing. The second question can be used answered through activation patching. We're actually using a slightly different method for this first question in our paper. We're going to use something called description generation, which works unsupervisedly. Basically, we're going to take activations and look at all contexts that have high similarity to the activation. For example, here, let's say it's like the recipe calls for three ounces of sugar or open the cookbook to page 38.
